title: Defer guaranteed work to WorkManager
description: Schedule deferrable, guaranteed background work through WorkManager with constraints, not raw threads that die with the process.
body: Use when work must complete eventually even across process death or reboot (sync, upload, cleanup). Do not start a bare thread or rely on the process staying alive, and do not use WorkManager for immediate in-screen UI work. Actions: enqueue a Worker with constraints (network, charging) and a backoff policy, make the work idempotent and resumable, and let the system run it when conditions are met. Validation signals include work completing after the app is backgrounded/killed and surviving a reboot. Risk boundary: a raw thread or one-off coroutine for guaranteed work is lost the moment the OS kills the process, silently dropping the task — durable background work needs a system-managed scheduler, while misusing it for foreground-immediate work adds latency.
